briefdescription.para: D-PHY level error handler.
Packet level error handler.
Protocol level error handler.
Short packet handler.
Frame received handler.
Virtual channel error handler.
Other error handler.

description.para: Register sets of MIPI CSI2 Rx Core

briefdescription.para: Core Configuration Register.
Protocol Configuration Register.
Core Status Register.
Global Interrupt Register.
Interrupt Status Register.
Interrupt Enable Register.
VC Selection Register.
Generic Short Packet Register.
VCx Frame Error Register.
Clock Lane Info Register.
Lane Information Registers.

detaileddescription.para: Lane 0 Info Register

briefdescription.para: Lane 1 Info Register.
Lane 2 Info Register.
Lane 3 Info Register.
Image Information Register for each Virtual Channel.

detaileddescription.para: Virtual Channel 0 Image Information 1 Register

briefdescription.para: Virtual Channel 0 Image Information 2 Register.
Virtual Channel 1 Image Information 1 Register.
Virtual Channel 1 Image Information 2 Register.
Virtual Channel 2 Image Information 1 Register.
Virtual Channel 2 Image Information 2 Register.
Virtual Channel 3 Image Information 1 Register.
Virtual Channel 3 Image Information 2 Register.
Virtual Channel 4 Image Information 1 Register.
Virtual Channel 4 Image Information 2 Register.
Virtual Channel 5 Image Information 1 Register.
Virtual Channel 5 Image Information 2 Register.
Virtual Channel 6 Image Information 1 Register.
Virtual Channel 6 Image Information 2 Register.
Virtual Channel 7 Image Information 1 Register.
Virtual Channel 7 Image Information 2 Register.
Virtual Channel 8 Image Information 1 Register.
Virtual Channel 8 Image Information 2 Register.
Virtual Channel 9 Image Information 1 Register.
Virtual Channel 9 Image Information 2 Register.
Virtual Channel 10 Image Information 1 Register.
Virtual Channel 10 Image Information 2 Register.
Virtual Channel 11 Image Information 1 Register.
Virtual Channel 11 Image Information 2 Register.
Virtual Channel 12 Image Information 1 Register.
Virtual Channel 12 Image Information 2 Register.
Virtual Channel 13 Image Information 1 Register.
Virtual Channel 13 Image Information 2 Register.
Virtual Channel 14 Image Information 1 Register.
Virtual Channel 14 Image Information 2 Register.
Virtual Channel 15 Image Information 1 Register.
Virtual Channel 15 Image Information 2 Register.

sectiondef.header: Bitmasks and offsets of XCSI_CCR_OFFSET register

description.para: This register is used for the enabling/disabling and resetting the core of CSI2 Rx Controller

briefdescription.para: Soft Reset the core.
Enable/Disable core.
Shift bits for Soft reset.
Shift bits for Core Enable.

sectiondef.header: Bitmasks and offset of XCSI_PCR_OFFSET register

description.para: This register reports the number of lanes configured during core generation and number of lanes actively used.

briefdescription.para: Maximum lanes in core.
Active lanes in core.
Shift bits for Max Lanes.
Shift bits for Active Lanes.

sectiondef.header: Bitmasks and offsets of XCSI_CSR_OFFSET register

description.para: This register captures the core's status.

briefdescription.para: 16-bit Packet Counter
Short Packet FIFO Full.
Short Packet FIFO Not Empty.
Stream Line Buffer Full.
Reset in Progress OR Core Disabled.
Shift bits for Packet Counter.
Shift bits for Short Packet FIFO Full.
Shift bits for Short Packet Not Empty.
Shift bits for Stream Line Buffer Full.
Bit Shift for Reset in Progress.

sectiondef.header: Bitmasks and offsets of XCSI_GIER_OFFSET register

description.para: This register contains the global interrupt enable bit.

briefdescription.para: Global Interrupt Enable bit.
Shift bits for Global Interrupt Enable.
Enable the Global Interrupts.
Disable the Global Interrupts.

sectiondef.header: Bitmasks and offsets of XCSI_ISR_OFFSET register

description.para: This register contains the interrupt status.

briefdescription.para: Frame Received.
VCX Frame Error.
Skewcalhs Error.
YUV420 Word count Error.
Word count corruption.
Incorrect Lanes Configured.
Short Packet FIFO FULL.
Short Packet FIFO Not Empty.
Stream Line Buffer Full.
Detect Stop State.
SoT Error.
SoT Sync Error.
ECC 2 bit Error.
ECC 1 bit Error.
CRC Error.
Unknown data ID packet Error.
Frame Sync Error on Virtual Channel 3.
Frame Level Error on Virtual Channel 3.
Frame Sync Error on Virtual Channel 2.
Frame Level Error on Virtual Channel 2.
Frame Sync Error on Virtual Channel 1.
Frame Level Error on Virtual Channel 1.
Frame Sync Error on Virtual Channel 0.
Frame Level Error on Virtual Channel 0.
All interrupts mask.
Shift bits for Frame received interrupt.
Shift bits for VCx Frame Error interrupt.
Shift bits for skeecalchs Error interrupt.
Shift bits for YUV420 word count interrupt.
Shift bits for Word Count corruption.
Shift bits for Incorrect Lanes configured.
Shift bits for Short Packet FIFO Full.
Shift bits for Short Packet FIFO Not Empty.
Shift bits for Stream Line Buffer Full.
Shift bits for Stop State.
Shift bits for Start of Transmission Error.
Shift bits for Start of Transmission Sync Error.
Shift bits for 2 bit ECC error.
Shift bits for 1 bit ECC error.
Shift bits for Packet CRC error.
Shift bits for Unsupported Data ID error.
Shift bits for Virtual Channel 3 Frame Synchronisation Error.
Shift bits for Virtual Channel 3 Frame Level Error.
Shift bits for Virtual Channel 2 Frame Synchronisation Error.
Shift bits for Virtual Channel 2 Frame Level Error.
Shift bits for Virtual Channel 1 Frame Synchronisation Error.
Shift bits for Virtual Channel 1 Frame Level Error.
Shift bits for Virtual Channel 0 Frame Synchronisation Error.
Shift bits for Virtual Channel 0 Frame Level Error.

description.para: The interrupts are grouped into DPHY Level Errors, Protocol Decoding Errors, Packet Level Errors, Normal Errors, Frame Received interrupt and Short Packet related.
These are used in XCsi_InterruptHandler() to determine the particular callback

memberdef.name: XCSI_INTR_PROT_MASK
memberdef.initializer: (XCSI_ISR_VC3FSYNCERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC3FLVLERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC2FSYNCERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC2FLVLERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC1FSYNCERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC1FLVLERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC0FSYNCERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC0FLVLERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VCXFE_MASK)

briefdescription.para: Protocol decoding error mask for VC0-3.
Frame Sync Error on Virtual Channel 15.
Frame Level Error on Virtual Channel 15.
Frame Sync Error on Virtual Channel 14.
Frame Level Error on Virtual Channel 14.
Frame Sync Error on Virtual Channel 13.
Frame Level Error on Virtual Channel 13.
Frame Sync Error on Virtual Channel 12.
Frame Level Error on Virtual Channel 12.
Frame Sync Error on Virtual Channel 11.
Frame Level Error on Virtual Channel 11.
Frame Sync Error on Virtual Channel 10.
Frame Level Error on Virtual Channel 10.
Frame Sync Error on Virtual Channel 9.
Frame Level Error on Virtual Channel 9.
Frame Sync Error on Virtual Channel 8.
Frame Level Error on Virtual Channel 8.
Frame Sync Error on Virtual Channel 7.
Frame Level Error on Virtual Channel 7.
Frame Sync Error on Virtual Channel 6.
Frame Level Error on Virtual Channel 6.
Frame Sync Error on Virtual Channel 5.
Frame Level Error on Virtual Channel 5.
Frame Sync Error on Virtual Channel 4.
Frame Level Error on Virtual Channel 4.

memberdef.name: XCSI_INTR_VCFE_MASK
memberdef.initializer: (XCSI_ISR_VC4FSYNCERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC4FLVLERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC5FSYNCERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC5FLVLERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC6FSYNCERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC6FLVLERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC7FSYNCERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC7FLVLERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC8FSYNCERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC8FLVLERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC9FSYNCERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC9FLVLERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC10FSYNCERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC10FLVLERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC11FSYNCERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC11FLVLERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC12FSYNCERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC12FLVLERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC13FSYNCERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC13FLVLERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC14FSYNCERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC14FLVLERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC15FSYNCERR_MASK | \ XCSI_ISR_VC15FLVLERR_MASK)

briefdescription.para: Virtual channel frame error mask for VC4-15.
Packet level error interrupt mask.
D-PHY level error interrupt mask.
Short packet FIFO interrupt mask.
Frame received interrupt mask.
VCX frame error interrupt mask.
Other error interrupt mask.

sectiondef.header: Bitmasks and offsets of XCSI_IER_OFFSET register

description.para: This register contains the interrupt enable masks

briefdescription.para: Frame Received.
VCX Frame Error.
Skewcalchs State.
YUV420 Word Count Error.
Word Count Corruption.
Incorrect Lanes Configured.
Short Packet FIFO FULL.
Short Packet FIFO Not Empty.
Stream Line Buffer Full.
Detect Stop State.
SoT Error.
SoT Sync Error.
ECC 2 bit Error.
ECC 1 bit Error.
CRC Error.
Unknown data ID packet Error.
Frame Sync Error on Virtual Channel 3.
Frame Level Error on Virtual Channel 3.
Frame Sync Error on Virtual Channel 2.
Frame Level Error on Virtual Channel 2.
Frame Sync Error on Virtual Channel 1.
Frame Level Error on Virtual Channel 1.
Frame Sync Error on Virtual Channel 0.
Frame Level Error on Virtual Channel 0.
All interrupts mask.
Shift bits for Frame received interrupt.
Shift bits for skewcalhs status.
Shift bits for YUV 420 Word Count error.
Shift bits for Word count corruption.
Shift bits for Incorrect Lanes configured.
Shift bits for Short Packet FIFO Full.
Shift bits for Short Packet FIFO Not Empty.
Shift bits for Stream Line Buffer Full.
Shift bits for Stop State.
Shift bits for Start of Transmission Error.
Shift bits for Start of Transmission Sync Error.
Shift bits for 2 bit ECC error.
Shift bits for 1 bit ECC error.
Shift bits for Packet CRC error.
Shift bits for Unsupported Data ID error.
Shift bits for Virtual Channel 3 Frame Synchronisation Error.
Shift bits for Virtual Channel 3 Frame Level Error.
Shift bits for Virtual Channel 2 Frame Synchronisation Error.
Shift bits for Virtual Channel 2 Frame Level Error.
Shift bits for Virtual Channel 1 Frame Synchronisation Error.
Shift bits for Virtual Channel 1 Frame Level Error.
Shift bits for Virtual Channel 0 Frame Synchronisation Error.
Shift bits for Virtual Channel 0 Frame Level Error.

sectiondef.header: Bitmasks and offsets of XCSI_SPKTR_OFFSET register

description.para: This register contains the masks for getting the 16 bit data, virtual channel and data type

briefdescription.para: 16 bit short packet data Received
Virtual channel number.
Data Type.
Shift bits for Short Packet Data.
Shift bits for Short Packet Virtual Channel Data.
Bit Shift for Short Packet Data Type.

sectiondef.header: Bitmasks and offsets of XCSI_CLKINFR_OFFSET register

description.para: This register contains the masks for getting current status of clock lane

briefdescription.para: Stop State on clock lane.
Shift bits for Clock Lane Stop bit.

sectiondef.header: Bitmasks and offsets of XCSI_L(0..3)INFR register

description.para: This register contains the masks for getting current status of data lanes.

briefdescription.para: Stop State on clock lane.
SkewcalHs State on clock lane.
Detection of ErrSoTHS.
Detection of ErrSoTSyncHS.
Bit Shift for Data Lane Stop State.
Bit Shift for Data Lane SkewCalHs State.
Bit Shift for Start of Transmission Error.
Bit Shift for Start of Transmission Sync Error.

sectiondef.header: Bitmasks and offsets of XCSI_VC(0..3)INF(1/2)R register

description.para: This register contains the masks to get line count, byte count from Info Reg 1 and to get current data type being processed from Info Reg 2 for each virtual channel.

briefdescription.para: Number of long packets written into line buffer.
Byte count of current packet in process.
Shift bits for Virtual Channel Line Count bits.
Shift bits for Virtual Channel Byte Count bits.
Data type of current packet.
Bit Shift for Virtual Channel Data Type.
Callback type for all interrupts defined.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a bit mask indicating the cause of the event. For current core version, this parameter is "OR" of 0 or more XCSI_ISR_*_MASK constants defined in xcsi_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: Stub callback function for asynchronous error interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: This routine is a stub for the asynchronous error interrupt callback.

Each of callback functions to be called on different types of interrupts. These stub functions are set during XCsi_CfgInitialize as default callback functions. If application is not registered any of the callback function, these functions will be called for doing nothing.
The stub is here in case the upper layer forgot to set the handler. On initialization, Error interrupt handler is set to this callback. It is considered an error for this handler to be invoked.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a bit mask indicating the cause of the error. Its value equals 'OR'ing one or more XCSI_ISR_*_MASK values defined in xcsi_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: Initialize the XCsi instance provided by the caller based on the given Config structure.

detaileddescription.para: Initialize the CSI driver.

InstancePtr
is the XCsi instance to operate on.
CfgPtr
is the device configuration structure containing information about a specific CSI.
EffectiveAddr
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.
XST_SUCCESS if initialization was successful.
None. This function initializes the CSI driver/device. This function must be called prior to using the driver. Initialization includes setting up the instance data and ensuring the hardware is in a quiescent state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
is a reference to a structure containing information about a specific XCsi instance.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if initialization was successful.
XST_FAILURE if initialization failed.

briefdescription.para: This function will do a reset of the IP.

detaileddescription.para: Reset the CSI controller.
This will reset the values of all registers except Core Config and Protocol Config registers.

InstancePtr
is the XCsi instance to operate on.
XST_SUCCESS on proper reset.
XST_FAILURE on timeout and core being stuck in reset.
None. This function resets the CSI controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if reset was successful.
XST_FAILURE if reset failed.

briefdescription.para: This function will enable/disable the IP Core to start processing.

detaileddescription.para: Activate or deactivate the CSI controller.

InstancePtr
is the XCsi instance to operate on.
Flag
will be used to indicate Enable or Disable action.
XST_SUCCESS on successful core enable or disable.
XST_FAILURE if core disable times out.
None. This function activates or deactivates the CSI controller based on the Flag parameter.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
is the enable/disable flag. Set to 1 to enable, 0 to disable.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if operation was successful.
XST_FAILURE if operation failed.

briefdescription.para: This function will configure the core with proper number of Active Lanes.

detaileddescription.para: Configure the CSI controller.

InstancePtr
is the XCsi instance to operate on.
XST_SUCCESS on configuring the core.
XST_FAILURE if active lanes not set correctly.
None. This function configures the CSI controller with the active lanes setting.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if configuration was successful.
XST_FAILURE if configuration failed.

briefdescription.para: This function will control the virtual channels selection dynamically.

detaileddescription.para: Set the virtual channel selection.

InstancePtr
is the XCsi instance to operate on.
Value
will set the virtual channels corresponding to each bit value. bit value 1: core processes packets with this VC, bit value 0: core filters packets with this VC.
None.
None. This function sets the virtual channel selection for filtering.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
is the virtual channel selection value to be set.

briefdescription.para: This function will return the virtual channels selected.

detaileddescription.para: Get the virtual channel selection.

InstancePtr
is the XCsi instance to operate on.
Value of selected VCs.
None. This function returns the current virtual channel selection.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.

briefdescription.para: This function will get the short packet received in the FIFO from the Generic Short Packet Register and fill up the structure passed from caller.

detaileddescription.para: Get short packet information.

InstancePtr
is the XCsi instance to operate on.
ShortPacketStruct
is going to be filled up by this function and returned to the caller.
None.
None. This function retrieves the short packet information from the controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
is a pointer to the structure where short packet data will be stored.

briefdescription.para: This function will get the information about the state of the Clock Lane.

detaileddescription.para: Get clock lane information.

InstancePtr
is the XCsi instance to operate on.
ClkLane
is going to be filled up by this function and returned to the caller.
None.
None. This function retrieves the clock lane information from the controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
is a pointer to the structure where clock lane info will be stored.

briefdescription.para: This function will get the information about the state of a Data Lane.

detaileddescription.para: Get data lane information.

InstancePtr
is the XCsi instance to operate on.
Lane
is the Lane number whose information is requested.
DataLane
is going to be filled up by this function and returned to the caller.
None.
None. This function retrieves the data lane information for a specific lane.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
is the lane number (0 to 3) for which to get information.
is a pointer to the structure where data lane info will be stored.

briefdescription.para: This function will get the line count, byte count and data type information about a Virtual Channel.

detaileddescription.para: Get virtual channel information.

InstancePtr
is the XCsi instance to operate on.
Vc
is the Virtual Channel number whose information is requested.
VCInfo
is going to be filled up by this function and returned to the caller.
None.
None. This function retrieves the virtual channel information.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
is the virtual channel number for which to get information.
is a pointer to the structure where virtual channel info will be stored.

briefdescription.para: This function checks the validity of the active lanes parameter.

detaileddescription.para: Check if active lane count is valid.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
ActiveLanesCount
is the lane count to check if valid.
1 if specified Active Lanes is valid.
0 otherwise, if the Active Lanes specified isn't valid as per spec and design.
None. This function validates if the specified active lane count is valid for the current configuration.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
is the active lane count to be validated.

listitem.para: 1 if the active lane count is valid.
0 if the active lane count is invalid.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set bit in a register.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP.
is offset where the register is present.
of bit to be set.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to reset bit in a register.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP.
is offset where the register is present.
of bit to be reset.

memberdef.argsstring: (UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegisterOffset, u32 BitMask, u32 BitShift)
memberdef.name: XCsi_GetBitField

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the value of bitfield from register.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP.
is offset where the register is present.
of bit fields to be retrieved.
is offset of the field in register.

simplesect.para: Bit Field Value in u32 format.

memberdef.argsstring: (UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegisterOffset, u32 BitMask, u32 BitShift, u32 Value)
memberdef.name: XCsi_SetBitField

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set the value of bitfield from register.

parameterdescription.para: is a base address of IP.
is offset where the register is present.
of bit fields to be retrieved.
is offset of the field in register.
is to be set. Passed in u32 format.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to start the soft reset process by setting the soft reset bit in the Core Configuration Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI2 instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to stop the soft reset process by resetting the soft reset bit in the Core Configuration Register.

detaileddescription.para: This is done usually after Reset in Progress is 0.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to stop the packet processing by resetting Enable core bit in the Core Configuration Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to start the packet processing by setting Enable core bit in the Core Configuration Register.

detaileddescription.para: This is done after the configuration of active lanes, interrupt masks, etc.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to check if the Core is enabled by checking the core enable bit.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: 1 - If the CSI core is enabled.
0 - If the CSI core is disabled.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the number of lanes configured in the IP.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Max number of lanes available in u32 format.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the actual number of lanes being used by the IP to communicate with a CSI2 Tx.

detaileddescription.para: This is lesser or equal to Max lanes.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Number of lanes being used in u32 format.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set the actual number of lanes to be used to communicate with a CSI2 Tx.

detaileddescription.para: This is lesser than or equal to Max lanes.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.
is number of lanes to be made active.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the number of long packets received.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to check if the Short Packet FIFO is full or not.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: 1 - If the Short Packet FIFO Full.
0 - If the Short Packet FIFO available.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to check if the Short Packet FIFO is not empty.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: 1 - If the Short Packet FIFO is not Empty.
0 - If the Short Packet FIFO is Empty.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to check if the Stream Line Buffer is full or not.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: 1 - If the Stream line buffer is full.
0 - If the Stream line buffer is not full.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to check if a reset is completed or is in progress.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: 1 - If the Soft reset operation is in progress.
0 - If the Soft reset operation is completed.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to enable the global interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: This is used after setting the interrupts mask before enabling the core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to disable the global interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: This is done after disabling the core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI Instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: Lookup the device configuration based on the base address.

detaileddescription.para: Look up the hardware configuration for a device instance.
This function returns a reference to an XCsi_Config structure based on the base address. This function will return a NULL pointer if the base address is not found.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device to lookup for.

simplesect.para: XCsi_Config reference if BaseAddress is found, NULL otherwise.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the device to lookup for

simplesect.para: The reference to the configuration record in the configuration table (in xcsi_g.c) corresponding to the Device ID or if not found,a NULL pointer is returned.

briefdescription.para: Run a self-test on the CSI controller.

detaileddescription.para: Runs a self-test on the driver/device.
This function runs a self-test on the CSI controller/device. The self-test checks for proper functionality of the controller.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
XST_SUCCESS if self-test was successful.
XST_FAILURE if self-test failed.
None. This test checks if the LaneCount present in register matches the one from the generated file.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if self-test was successful
XST_FAILURE if the read value was not equal to _g.c file

briefdescription.para: Interrupt handler for the CSI controller.

detaileddescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the CSI2 Rx core.
This function is the interrupt handler for the CSI driver. It processes all pending interrupts and routes them to the appropriate callback handlers.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XCsi instance (passed as void *).
None.
This function should be connected to the interrupt system. This handler reads the pending interrupt from the Interrupt Status register, determines the source of the interrupts and calls the respective callbacks for the interrupts that are enabled in Interrupt Enable register, and finally clears the interrupts.

The application is responsible for connecting this function to the interrupt system. Application beyond this core is also responsible for providing callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XCsi_SetCallBack() during initialization phase.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi core instance.

simplesect.para: Interrupt should be enabled to execute interrupt handler.

briefdescription.para: Set callback function for interrupt handling.

detaileddescription.para: This routine installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:
This function sets a callback function for a specific interrupt handler type. The registered callback function will be called when the corresponding interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.

is the type of handler to register. It can be one of:
XCSI_HANDLER_DPHY
XCSI_HANDLER_PKTLVL
XCSI_HANDLER_PROTLVL
XCSI_HANDLER_SHORTPACKET
XCSI_HANDLER_FRAMERECVD
XCSI_HANDLER_VCXERR
XCSI_HANDLER_OTHERERROR
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if callback was successfully registered.
XST_FAILURE if callback registration failed.

para.preformatted: HandlerType Callback Function Type ---------------------------- -------------------------------------------- (XCSI_HANDLER_DPHY) DPhyLvlErrCallBack (XCSI_HANDLER_PROTLVL) ProtDecodeErrCallBack (XCSI_HANDLER_PKTLVL) PktLvlErrCallBack (XCSI_HANDLER_SHORTPACKET) ShortPacketCallBack (XCSI_HANDLER_FRAMERECVD) FrameRecvdCallBack (XCSI_HANDLER_VCXERR) VCXErrCallBack (XCSI_HANDLER_OTHERERROR) ErrorCallBack

parameterdescription.para: is the XCsi instance to operate on
is the type of call back to be registered.
is the pointer to a call back function which is called when a particular event occurs.
is a void pointer to data to be referenced to by the Callbackfunc

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS when handler is installed.
XST_INVALID_PARAM when HandlerType is invalid.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: Get the current interrupt enable mask.

detaileddescription.para: This function will get the interrupt mask set (enabled) in the CSI2 Rx core.
This function returns the current interrupt enable mask.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
is the XCsi instance to operate on

simplesect.para: Interrupt Mask with bits set for corresponding interrupt in Interrupt enable register

briefdescription.para: Enable specific interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: This function will enable the interrupts present in the interrupt mask passed onto the function.
This function enables the specified interrupts in the CSI controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
is the bit-mask of the interrupts to be enabled.
is the XCsi instance to operate on
is the interrupt mask which need to be enabled in core

briefdescription.para: Disable specific interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: This function will disable the interrupts present in the interrupt mask passed onto the function.
This function disables the specified interrupts in the CSI controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
is the bit-mask of the interrupts to be disabled.
is the XCsi instance to operate on
is the interrupt mask which need to be enabled in core

briefdescription.para: Get the interrupt status.

detaileddescription.para: This function will get the list of interrupts pending in the Interrupt Status Register of the CSI2 Rx core.
This function returns the current interrupt status register value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
is the XCsi instance to operate on

simplesect.para: Interrupt Mask with bits set for corresponding interrupt in Interrupt Status register

briefdescription.para: Clear specific interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: This function will clear the interrupts set in the Interrupt Status Register of the CSI2 Rx core.
This function clears the specified interrupts in the CSI controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
is the bit-mask of the interrupts to be cleared.
is the XCsi instance to operate on
is Interrupt Mask with bits set for corresponding interrupt to be cleared in the Interrupt Status register

briefdescription.para: Static inline function to read data from CSI register space.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of CSI
is the register offset.

briefdescription.para: Static inline function to write data to CSI register space.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of CSI
is the register offset.
is the value to be written to the register.

briefdescription.para: Timeout count for CSI soft reset.
Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
Flag denoting enabling of CSI.
Flag denoting disabling of CSI.
Max Lanes supported by CSI.
Max Virtual Channels supported for v1.0.
Max Virtual Channels supported for v2.0.
Max Virtual Channels supported for CSI.
Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
This file implements the functions to control and get info from the CSI2 RX Controller.

detaileddescription.para: Look up the hardware settings using device ID.
Contains diagnostic/self-test functions for the CSI Rx Controller core.
This file implements the functions which handle the interrupts and callbacks in the CSI2 Rx Controller.
Hardware register & masks definition file.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 vsa 06/17/15 Initial release 1.1 sss 08/17/16 Added 64 bit support

detaileddescription.para: It defines the register interface.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 vsa 06/15/15 Initial release 1.1 sss 08/17/16 Added 64 bit support 1.2 vsa 03/02/17 Add Word Count corruption interrupt bit support 1.5 vsa 08/10/20 Add YUV 420 8bit support

detaileddescription.para: The callbacks are registered for events which are interrupts clubbed together on the basis of the CSI specification.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 vsa 07/28/15 Initial release

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 vsa 06/18/15 Initial release

detaileddescription.para: The hardware setting is inside the configuration table in xcsi_g.c, generated automatically by XPS or manually by the user.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 vsa 06/18/15 Initial release
